category|Category:Otsuki-jinja}} [[JA:小槻神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11462132}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Otsuki Shrine |Image = [[File:Kotsuki Shrine Precincts.JPG|270px]]
Oratory (right)/Main shrine (back left) |Location = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Kusatsu City|Q386677|WD=Q386677}}873 Aochicho |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|35|0|3.86|N|135|58|38.89|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotsuki Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Rakubetsuou|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Ochibetsu no Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amagoyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = Sangensha {{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}{{ill|lt=cypress bark roof|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} |Alternative name = Ikenomiya (Ikenomiya) |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=May 3rd|Q2547|WD=Q2547}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Otsuki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kotsuki Shrine Torii.JPG|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Otsuki Shrine'' (Otsuki Shrine {{Sfn|Otsuki Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}/Otsugi Jinja {{Sfn|Kotsuki Taisha/Kotsuki Shrine (Gods)|1986}}) is located in Aochi Town, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Kusatsu City|Q386677|WD=Q386677}}. In {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Divine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} is "Kakudari wisteria" [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=341 Otsuki Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Agency). === Enshrined God === The following two deities are enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Rakubetsuou|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Ochibetsu no Mikoto}} (Ochiwake no Mikoto) *: Also written as ``Rakubetsu no Mikoto'' or ``Sobetsu no Mikoto.'' He was the 11th {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}{{ill|lt=prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381}} and the ancestor of {{ill|lt=Kotsukiyama-kun|Q11462129|WD=Q11462129}} ({{ill|lt=Kotsuki clan|Q11462133|WD=Q11462133}}). Depending on the literature, the notation ``{{ill|lt=Issokubetsu no Mikoto|Q11492324|WD=Q11492324}}'' is also seen, but Issokubetsu no Mikoto is a half-brother of Ochibetsu no Mikoto. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amagoyane Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Amenokoyane Mikoto) *: {{ill|lt=Enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}} in {{ill|lt=Medieval|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}. He is the ancestral deity of the Fujiwara clan, and it has been suggested that there is a connection with the fact that the adjacent Kanakatsusho was the territory of {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559}} (Fujiwara clan's guardian deity) {{Sfn|Kotsuki Taisha/Kotsuki Shrine (Gods)|1986}}. === History === {{ill|lt=Ancient|Q41493|WD=Q41493}} in {{ill|lt=Kurita County|Q6446084|WD=Q6446084}} (current {{ill|lt=Kusatsu City|Q386677|WD=Q386677}}, {{jalink|Ritto City|Q It is said that the shrine was founded by {{ill|lt=Otsukiyama-kun|Q11462129|WD=Q11462129}} (Kozukiyama-kō), a powerful family from the 857310}} area, who enshrined {{ill|lt=Rakubetsu-ō|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Ochibetsu-no-Mikoto}} as the ancestral deity. Since Otsukiyama-kun presented {{ill|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)|Q30253177|WD=Q30253177|lt=Imperial Court}} with {{ill|lt=Ume|Q11644506|WD=Q11644506}}, it is assumed that he belonged to a family in the Kurita County {{ill|lt=Gunji|Q4152149|WD=Q4152149}} class. Otsukiyama-kun is {{ill|lt=Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan}} in {{ill|lt=Heiankyo|Q751907|WD=Q751907|lt=Kyoto}} in 15 years ({{ill|lt=873|Q30369|WD=Q30369}}) He later moved to the {{ill|lt=Kotsuki clan|Q11462133|WD=Q11462133}} ({{ill|lt=official|Q11452088|WD=Q11452088}} family) and became a member of the {{jalink|Imperial court}. (Japan)|Q30253177|Served the Imperial Court}}.{{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho}} is {{ill|lt=Omi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}{{jali nk|Kurita County|Q6446084}} is described as "Otsuki Shrine", and related shrines {{ill|lt=Kotsuki Taisha|Q11462126|WD=Q11462126}} ({{jalin {{Sfn|Kotsuki Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} along with Shimotoyama). The company name is pronounced ``Wotsukino''. In the shrine biography, it is said that in the 2nd year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714}}) he was given the proclamation of ``Shoichii Otsuki Daimyojin'', and also that {{ill|lt=Tentoku (Japan)|Q1327662|WD=Q1327662|lt=Tentoku}} In 3rd year ({{ill|lt=959|Q23750|WD=Q23750}}), it was relocated to the current location near Shizuike Pond, and it is said that it was called ``Ikenomiya'' (Ikenomiya), in contrast to ``Kozuenomiya'' of Kotsuki Taisha Shrine. The shrine buildings and sacred treasures were burnt down twice in {{ill|lt=Enkei (Japan)|Q1327068|WD=Q1327068|lt=Enkei}} and {{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397}}. {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}} to {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=During the Sengoku period}}, the shrine was worshiped by the {{ill|lt=Aochi clan|Q11661125|WD=Q11661125}}, and the shrine was donated and repaired, and it is said that a mikoshi was carried around the castle during the {{ill|lt=regular festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}}.Also, according to the shrine's legend, it has a close relationship with {{ill|lt=Koshioi Shrine|Q11462224|WD=Q11462224}} in Kusatsu City (Oji, Kusatsu City). Among them, {{ill|lt=Sadaharu|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153}}3 years ({{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shohei}} Since the construction of the mikoshi in {{ill|lt=1364|Q6185|WD=Q6185}} in 19, the mikoshi has traveled to Otsuki Taisha Shrine, Otsuki Taisha Shrine, and Koshioi Shrine during the annual festival, but when it was abolished, it is said that the sacred treasure of Koshioi Shrine, the bottle, was shared as proof of the relationship. [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=339 Koshioi Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Agency). The one at Otsuki Shrine was later destroyed in a fire, but it is said that it is still considered a sacred treasure at Koshioi Shrine. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}), {{ill|lt=Modern company ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}, and after extensive construction in Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}}), it was promoted to {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} in Meiji 16 ({{ill|lt=1883|Q7816|WD=Q7816}}). The current shrine building was constructed in 1896. === Precincts === The precincts adjoin the ruins of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aochi Castle|1=UNKNOWN}} from the Sengoku period. The main shrine is a three-room shrine {{ill|lt=nagaru-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}{{ill|lt=hinoki bark|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}}, with a frontage of three rooms and a depth of two rooms. It was constructed in 1896. The worship hall is {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}}, and has a frontage of three rooms and a depth of three rooms. It is also said that there were a group of ancient tombs within the precincts, and stone walls were built using the stones excavated from the tombs. In addition, the tree that is 18 meters tall and estimated to be over 100 years old is designated as a famous tree of Kusatsu City. Otsuki Shrine Main Hall.JPG|Main Hall Otsuki Shrine Hall of Worship.JPG | Hall of Worship Otsuki Shrine Stone wall of the ancient tomb.JPG|Stone wall of the ancient tomb Shizu Pond.JPG | Shizu Pond === Setsusha === === Precinct Company === * Ebisu Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshiro Nushikami|1=UNKNOWN}}. Inside Tamagaki. * Kumano Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagison|1=UNKNOWN}}. Inside Tamagaki. * Hiyoshi Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Oyama Kuijin|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105}}. Inside Tamagaki. * Kifune Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Takamikami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takayokujin}}. {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} June 6th year ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707}}). Inside Tamagaki. * Ryuosha - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Watatsumi|Q1474187|WD=Q1474187|lt=Toyota Hikokami}}. It is said that in ancient times people would immerse the company's statue of the Dragon King in Shizu Pond to pray for rain. * Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}. {{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 5th year ({{ill|lt=863 years|Q25052|WD=Q25052}}) Kansho on August 15th. It is enshrined together with Nogami Shrine and is located near the torii gate. * Nogami Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Kayanohime|Q8191878|WD=Q8191878|lt=Kusanohime}}. It is enshrined together with Hachiman Shrine and is located near the torii gate. === Kyougaisha === * Hachimangu Shrine (Hachiman Shrine) ** Enshrinement location: Baba-cho, Kusatsu City ** Annual festival: {{ill|lt=January 10th|Q2243|WD=Q2243}} * Wakamiya Shrine ** Enshrinement location: Okamoto-cho, Kusatsu City ({{Coord|35|00|04.17|N|135|58|52.19|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Outside Shrine: Wakamiya Shrine}}) ** Enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Eight Wills and Life|1=UNKNOWN}} * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei Shrine) ** Enshrinement location: Aochi-cho, Kusatsu City (Shizuikewaki, {{Wiki coordinates|35|00|12.38|N|135|58|35.24|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|location|name=Shinmei Shrine}}) ** Enshrined deity: Owahime no Mikoto (same as {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}}) Wakamiya Shrine (Otsuki Shrine outside the precincts).JPG|Wakamiya Shrine (outside the precincts) Shinmei Shrine (Otsuki Shrine outside the precincts).JPG|Shinmei Shrine (outside the precincts of the shrine) === Festival ===
List of annual festivals
If there is no mention of large festival or middle festival, it is a small festival. * January ** Saitan Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1st|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) - Middle Festival. ** Dragon God Festival ({{ill|lt=January 15th|Q2260|WD=Q2260}}) *February ** Setsubun Festival ({{ill|lt=February 3rd|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}) - Middle Festival. ** Kigen Festival ({{ill|lt=February 11th|Q2335|WD=Q2335}}) ** Prayer Festival ({{ill|lt=February 23rd|Q2352|WD=Q2352}}) - Grand festival. *April ** Sakaki Festival ({{ill|lt=April 29th|Q2535|WD=Q2535}}) * May ** Yoimiya Festival ({{ill|lt=May 2nd|Q2545|WD=Q2545}}) ** Reitaisai ({{ill|lt=May 3rd|Q2547|WD=Q2547}}) - Taisai. ** Shinko Festival (May 3rd) **Saida Festival * June ** Summer Purification ({{ill|lt=June 30th|Q2657|WD=Q2657}}) * October ** Early Grain Festival ({{ill|lt=October 17th|Q2962|WD=Q2962}}) ** Kakeho Festival *November ** Shichigosan Festival ({{ill|lt=November 15th|Q2982|WD=Q2982}})** Niinamesai ({{ill|lt=November 23rd|Q3021|WD=Q3021}}) - Big festival. *December ** Cannabis card distribution ceremony (early December) ** Great Purification Festival ({{ill|lt=December 31st|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) ** New Year's Eve Festival ({{ill|lt=December 31st|Q2912|WD=Q2912}})

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amagoyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = Sangensha {{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}{{ill|lt=cypress bark roof|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} |Alternative name = Ikenomiya (Ikenomiya) |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=May 3rd|Q2547|WD=Q2547}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Otsuki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kotsuki Shrine Torii.JPG|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Otsuki Shrine'' (Otsuki Shrine {{Sfn|Otsuki Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}/Otsugi Jinja {{Sfn|Kotsuki Taisha/Kotsuki Shrine (Gods)|1986}}) is located in Aochi Town, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Kusatsu City|Q386677|WD=Q386677}}. In {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Divine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} is "Kakudari wisteria" [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=341 Otsuki Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Agency). === Enshrined God === The following two deities are enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Rakubetsuou|UNKNOWN|lt=Ochibetsu no Mikoto}} (Ochiwake no Mikoto) *: Also written as ``Rakubetsu no Mikoto'' or ``Sobetsu no Mikoto.'' He was the 11th {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}{{ill|lt=prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381}} and the ancestor of {{ill|lt=Kotsukiyama-kun|Q11462129|WD=Q11462129}} ({{ill|lt=Kotsuki clan|Q11462133|WD=Q11462133}}). Depending on the literature, the notation ``{{ill|lt=Issokubetsu no Mikoto|Q11492324|WD=Q11492324}}'' is also seen, but Issokubetsu no Mikoto is a half-brother of Ochibetsu no Mikoto. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amagoyane Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Amenokoyane Mikoto) *: {{ill|lt=Enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}} in {{ill|lt=Medieval|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}. He is the ancestral deity of the Fujiwara clan, and it has been suggested that there is a connection with the fact that the adjacent Kanakatsusho was the territory of {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559}} (Fujiwara clan's guardian deity) {{Sfn|Kotsuki Taisha/Kotsuki Shrine (Gods)|1986}}. === History === {{ill|lt=Ancient|Q41493|WD=Q41493}} in {{ill|lt=Kurita County|Q6446084|WD=Q6446084}} (current {{ill|lt=Kusatsu City|Q386677|WD=Q386677}}, {{jalink|Ritto City|Q It is said that the shrine was founded by {{ill|lt=Otsukiyama-kun|Q11462129|WD=Q11462129}} (Kozukiyama-kō), a powerful family from the 857310}} area, who enshrined {{ill|lt=Rakubetsu-ō|UNKNOWN|lt=Ochibetsu-no-Mikoto}} as the ancestral deity. Since Otsukiyama-kun presented {{ill|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)|Q30253177|WD=Q30253177|lt=Imperial Court}} with {{ill|lt=Ume|Q11644506|WD=Q11644506}}, it is assumed that he belonged to a family in the Kurita County {{ill|lt=Gunji|Q4152149|WD=Q4152149}} class. Otsukiyama-kun is {{ill|lt=Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan}} in {{ill|lt=Heiankyo|Q751907|WD=Q751907|lt=Kyoto}} in 15 years ({{ill|lt=873|Q30369|WD=Q30369}}) He later moved to the {{ill|lt=Kotsuki clan|Q11462133|WD=Q11462133}} ({{ill|lt=official|Q11452088|WD=Q11452088}} family) and became a member of the {{jalink|Imperial court}. (Japan)|Q30253177|Served the Imperial Court}}.{{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho}} is {{ill|lt=Omi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}{{jali nk|Kurita County|Q6446084}} is described as "Otsuki Shrine", and related shrines {{ill|lt=Kotsuki Taisha|Q11462126|WD=Q11462126}} ({{jalin {{Sfn|Kotsuki Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} along with Shimotoyama). The company name is pronounced ``Wotsukino''. In the shrine biography, it is said that in the 2nd year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714}}) he was given the proclamation of ``Shoichii Otsuki Daimyojin'', and also that {{ill|lt=Tentoku (Japan)|Q1327662|WD=Q1327662|lt=Tentoku}} In 3rd year ({{ill|lt=959|Q23750|WD=Q23750}}), it was relocated to the current location near Shizuike Pond, and it is said that it was called ``Ikenomiya'' (Ikenomiya), in contrast to ``Kozuenomiya'' of Kotsuki Taisha Shrine. The shrine buildings and sacred treasures were burnt down twice in {{ill|lt=Enkei (Japan)|Q1327068|WD=Q1327068|lt=Enkei}} and {{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397}}. {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}} to {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=During the Sengoku period}}, the shrine was worshiped by the {{ill|lt=Aochi clan|Q11661125|WD=Q11661125}}, and the shrine was donated and repaired, and it is said that a mikoshi was carried around the castle during the {{ill|lt=regular festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}}.Also, according to the shrine's legend, it has a close relationship with {{ill|lt=Koshioi Shrine|Q11462224|WD=Q11462224}} in Kusatsu City (Oji, Kusatsu City). Among them, {{ill|lt=Sadaharu|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153}}3 years ({{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shohei}} Since the construction of the mikoshi in {{ill|lt=1364|Q6185|WD=Q6185}} in 19, the mikoshi has traveled to Otsuki Taisha Shrine, Otsuki Taisha Shrine, and Koshioi Shrine during the annual festival, but when it was abolished, it is said that the sacred treasure of Koshioi Shrine, the bottle, was shared as proof of the relationship. [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=339 Koshioi Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Agency). The one at Otsuki Shrine was later destroyed in a fire, but it is said that it is still considered a sacred treasure at Koshioi Shrine. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}), {{ill|lt=Modern company ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}, and after extensive construction in Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}}), it was promoted to {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} in Meiji 16 ({{ill|lt=1883|Q7816|WD=Q7816}}). The current shrine building was constructed in 1896. === Precincts === The precincts adjoin the ruins of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aochi Castle|1=UNKNOWN}} from the Sengoku period. The main shrine is a three-room shrine {{ill|lt=nagaru-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}{{ill|lt=hinoki bark|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}}, with a frontage of three rooms and a depth of two rooms. It was constructed in 1896. The worship hall is {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}}, and has a frontage of three rooms and a depth of three rooms. It is also said that there were a group of ancient tombs within the precincts, and stone walls were built using the stones excavated from the tombs. In addition, the tree that is 18 meters tall and estimated to be over 100 years old is designated as a famous tree of Kusatsu City. Inside Tamagaki. * Kumano Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagison|1=UNKNOWN}}. Inside Tamagaki. * Hiyoshi Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Oyama Kuijin|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105}}. Inside Tamagaki. * Kifune Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Takamikami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takayokujin}}. {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} June 6th year ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707}}). Inside Tamagaki. * Ryuosha - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Watatsumi|Q1474187|WD=Q1474187|lt=Toyota Hikokami}}. It is said that in ancient times people would immerse the company's statue of the Dragon King in Shizu Pond to pray for rain. * Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}. {{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 5th year ({{ill|lt=863 years|Q25052|WD=Q25052}}) Kansho on August 15th. It is enshrined together with Nogami Shrine and is located near the torii gate. * Nogami Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Kayanohime|Q8191878|WD=Q8191878|lt=Kusanohime}}. It is enshrined together with Hachiman Shrine and is located near the torii gate. === Kyougaisha === * Hachimangu Shrine (Hachiman Shrine) ** Enshrinement location: Baba-cho, Kusatsu City ** Annual festival: {{ill|lt=January 10th|Q2243|WD=Q2243}} * Wakamiya Shrine ** Enshrinement location: Okamoto-cho, Kusatsu City ({{Coord|35|00|04.17|N|135|58|52.19|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Outside Shrine: Wakamiya Shrine}}) ** Enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Eight Wills and Life|1=UNKNOWN}} * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei Shrine) ** Enshrinement location: Aochi-cho, Kusatsu City (Shizuikewaki, {{Wiki coordinates|35|00|12.38|N|135|58|35.24|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|location|name=Shinmei Shrine}}) ** Enshrined deity: Owahime no Mikoto (same as {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}}) Wakamiya Shrine (Otsuki Shrine outside the precincts).JPG|Wakamiya Shrine (outside the precincts) Shinmei Shrine (Otsuki Shrine outside the precincts).JPG|Shinmei Shrine (outside the precincts of the shrine) === Festival ===
List of annual festivals
If there is no mention of large festival or middle festival, it is a small festival. * January ** Saitan Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1st|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) - Middle Festival. ** Dragon God Festival ({{ill|lt=January 15th|Q2260|WD=Q2260}}) *February ** Setsubun Festival ({{ill|lt=February 3rd|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}) - Middle Festival. ** Kigen Festival ({{ill|lt=February 11th|Q2335|WD=Q2335}}) ** Prayer Festival ({{ill|lt=February 23rd|Q2352|WD=Q2352}}) - Grand festival. *April ** Sakaki Festival ({{ill|lt=April 29th|Q2535|WD=Q2535}}) * May ** Yoimiya Festival ({{ill|lt=May 2nd|Q2545|WD=Q2545}}) ** Reitaisai ({{ill|lt=May 3rd|Q2547|WD=Q2547}}) - Taisai. ** Shinko Festival (May 3rd) **Saida Festival * June ** Summer Purification ({{ill|lt=June 30th|Q2657|WD=Q2657}}) * October ** Early Grain Festival ({{ill|lt=October 17th|Q2962|WD=Q2962}}) ** Kakeho Festival *November ** Shichigosan Festival ({{ill|lt=November 15th|Q2982|WD=Q2982}})** Niinamesai ({{ill|lt=November 23rd|Q3021|WD=Q3021}}) - Big festival. *December ** Cannabis card distribution ceremony (early December) ** Great Purification Festival ({{ill|lt=December 31st|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) ** New Year's Eve Festival ({{ill|lt=December 31st|Q2912|WD=Q2912}})

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amagoyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = Sangensha {{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}{{ill|lt=cypress bark roof|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} |Alternative name = Ikenomiya (Ikenomiya) |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=May 3rd|Q2547|WD=Q2547}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Otsuki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kotsuki Shrine Torii.JPG|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Otsuki Shrine'' (Otsuki Shrine {{Sfn|Otsuki Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}/Otsugi Jinja {{Sfn|Kotsuki Taisha/Kotsuki Shrine (Gods)|1986}}) is located in Aochi Town, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Kusatsu City|Q386677|WD=Q386677}}. In {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Divine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} is "Kakudari wisteria" [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=341 Otsuki Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Agency). === Enshrined God === The following two deities are enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Rakubetsuou|UNKNOWN|lt=Ochibetsu no Mikoto}} (Ochiwake no Mikoto) *: Also written as ``Rakubetsu no Mikoto'' or ``Sobetsu no Mikoto.'' He was the 11th {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}{{ill|lt=prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381}} and the ancestor of {{ill|lt=Kotsukiyama-kun|Q11462129|WD=Q11462129}} ({{ill|lt=Kotsuki clan|Q11462133|WD=Q11462133}}). Depending on the literature, the notation ``{{ill|lt=Issokubetsu no Mikoto|Q11492324|WD=Q11492324}}'' is also seen, but Issokubetsu no Mikoto is a half-brother of Ochibetsu no Mikoto. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amagoyane Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Amenokoyane Mikoto) *: {{ill|lt=Enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}} in {{ill|lt=Medieval|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}. He is the ancestral deity of the Fujiwara clan, and it has been suggested that there is a connection with the fact that the adjacent Kanakatsusho was the territory of {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559}} (Fujiwara clan's guardian deity) {{Sfn|Kotsuki Taisha/Kotsuki Shrine (Gods)|1986}}. === History === {{ill|lt=Ancient|Q41493|WD=Q41493}} in {{ill|lt=Kurita County|Q6446084|WD=Q6446084}} (current {{ill|lt=Kusatsu City|Q386677|WD=Q386677}}, {{jalink|Ritto City|Q It is said that the shrine was founded by {{ill|lt=Otsukiyama-kun|Q11462129|WD=Q11462129}} (Kozukiyama-kō), a powerful family from the 857310}} area, who enshrined {{ill|lt=Rakubetsu-ō|UNKNOWN|lt=Ochibetsu-no-Mikoto}} as the ancestral deity. Since Otsukiyama-kun presented {{ill|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)|Q30253177|WD=Q30253177|lt=Imperial Court}} with {{ill|lt=Ume|Q11644506|WD=Q11644506}}, it is assumed that he belonged to a family in the Kurita County {{ill|lt=Gunji|Q4152149|WD=Q4152149}} class. Otsukiyama-kun is {{ill|lt=Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan}} in {{ill|lt=Heiankyo|Q751907|WD=Q751907|lt=Kyoto}} in 15 years ({{ill|lt=873|Q30369|WD=Q30369}}) He later moved to the {{ill|lt=Kotsuki clan|Q11462133|WD=Q11462133}} ({{ill|lt=official|Q11452088|WD=Q11452088}} family) and became a member of the {{jalink|Imperial court}. (Japan)|Q30253177|Served the Imperial Court}}.{{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho}} is {{ill|lt=Omi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}{{jali nk|Kurita County|Q6446084}} is described as "Otsuki Shrine", and related shrines {{ill|lt=Kotsuki Taisha|Q11462126|WD=Q11462126}} ({{jalin {{Sfn|Kotsuki Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} along with Shimotoyama). The company name is pronounced ``Wotsukino''. In the shrine biography, it is said that in the 2nd year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714}}) he was given the proclamation of ``Shoichii Otsuki Daimyojin'', and also that {{ill|lt=Tentoku (Japan)|Q1327662|WD=Q1327662|lt=Tentoku}} In 3rd year ({{ill|lt=959|Q23750|WD=Q23750}}), it was relocated to the current location near Shizuike Pond, and it is said that it was called ``Ikenomiya'' (Ikenomiya), in contrast to ``Kozuenomiya'' of Kotsuki Taisha Shrine. The shrine buildings and sacred treasures were burnt down twice in {{ill|lt=Enkei (Japan)|Q1327068|WD=Q1327068|lt=Enkei}} and {{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397}}. {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}} to {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=During the Sengoku period}}, the shrine was worshiped by the {{ill|lt=Aochi clan|Q11661125|WD=Q11661125}}, and the shrine was donated and repaired, and it is said that a mikoshi was carried around the castle during the {{ill|lt=regular festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}}.Also, according to the shrine's legend, it has a close relationship with {{ill|lt=Koshioi Shrine|Q11462224|WD=Q11462224}} in Kusatsu City (Oji, Kusatsu City). Among them, {{ill|lt=Sadaharu|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153}}3 years ({{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shohei}} Since the construction of the mikoshi in {{ill|lt=1364|Q6185|WD=Q6185}} in 19, the mikoshi has traveled to Otsuki Taisha Shrine, Otsuki Taisha Shrine, and Koshioi Shrine during the annual festival, but when it was abolished, it is said that the sacred treasure of Koshioi Shrine, the bottle, was shared as proof of the relationship. [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=339 Koshioi Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Agency). The one at Otsuki Shrine was later destroyed in a fire, but it is said that it is still considered a sacred treasure at Koshioi Shrine. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}), {{ill|lt=Modern company ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}, and after extensive construction in Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}}), it was promoted to {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} in Meiji 16 ({{ill|lt=1883|Q7816|WD=Q7816}}). The current shrine building was constructed in 1896. === Precincts === The precincts adjoin the ruins of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aochi Castle|1=UNKNOWN}} from the Sengoku period. The main shrine is a three-room shrine {{ill|lt=nagaru-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}{{ill|lt=hinoki bark|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}}, with a frontage of three rooms and a depth of two rooms. It was constructed in 1896. The worship hall is {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}}, and has a frontage of three rooms and a depth of three rooms. It is also said that there were a group of ancient tombs within the precincts, and stone walls were built using the stones excavated from the tombs. In addition, the tree that is 18 meters tall and estimated to be over 100 years old is designated as a famous tree of Kusatsu City. Inside Tamagaki. * Kumano Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagison|1=UNKNOWN}}. Inside Tamagaki. * Hiyoshi Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Oyama Kuijin|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105}}. Inside Tamagaki. * Kifune Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Takamikami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takayokujin}}. {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} June 6th year ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707}}). Inside Tamagaki. * Ryuosha - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Watatsumi|Q1474187|WD=Q1474187|lt=Toyota Hikokami}}. It is said that in ancient times people would immerse the company's statue of the Dragon King in Shizu Pond to pray for rain. * Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}. {{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 5th year ({{ill|lt=863 years|Q25052|WD=Q25052}}) Kansho on August 15th. It is enshrined together with Nogami Shrine and is located near the torii gate. * Nogami Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Kayanohime|Q8191878|WD=Q8191878|lt=Kusanohime}}. It is enshrined together with Hachiman Shrine and is located near the torii gate. === Kyougaisha === * Hachimangu Shrine (Hachiman Shrine) ** Enshrinement location: Baba-cho, Kusatsu City ** Annual festival: {{ill|lt=January 10th|Q2243|WD=Q2243}} * Wakamiya Shrine ** Enshrinement location: Okamoto-cho, Kusatsu City ({{Coord|35|00|04.17|N|135|58|52.19|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Outside Shrine: Wakamiya Shrine}}) ** Enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Eight Wills and Life|1=UNKNOWN}} * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei Shrine) ** Enshrinement location: Aochi-cho, Kusatsu City (Shizuikewaki, {{Wiki coordinates|35|00|12.38|N|135|58|35.24|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|location|name=Shinmei Shrine}}) ** Enshrined deity: Owahime no Mikoto (same as {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}}) Wakamiya Shrine (Otsuki Shrine outside the precincts).JPG|Wakamiya Shrine (outside the precincts) Shinmei Shrine (Otsuki Shrine outside the precincts).JPG|Shinmei Shrine (outside the precincts of the shrine) === Festival ===
List of annual festivals
If there is no mention of large festival or middle festival, it is a small festival. * January ** Saitan Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1st|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) - Middle Festival. ** Dragon God Festival ({{ill|lt=January 15th|Q2260|WD=Q2260}}) *February ** Setsubun Festival ({{ill|lt=February 3rd|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}) - Middle Festival. ** Kigen Festival ({{ill|lt=February 11th|Q2335|WD=Q2335}}) ** Prayer Festival ({{ill|lt=February 23rd|Q2352|WD=Q2352}}) - Grand festival. *April ** Sakaki Festival ({{ill|lt=April 29th|Q2535|WD=Q2535}}) * May ** Yoimiya Festival ({{ill|lt=May 2nd|Q2545|WD=Q2545}}) ** Reitaisai ({{ill|lt=May 3rd|Q2547|WD=Q2547}}) - Taisai. ** Shinko Festival (May 3rd) **Saida Festival * June ** Summer Purification ({{ill|lt=June 30th|Q2657|WD=Q2657}}) * October ** Early Grain Festival ({{ill|lt=October 17th|Q2962|WD=Q2962}}) ** Kakeho Festival *November ** Shichigosan Festival ({{ill|lt=November 15th|Q2982|WD=Q2982}})** Niinamesai ({{ill|lt=November 23rd|Q3021|WD=Q3021}}) - Big festival. *December ** Cannabis card distribution ceremony (early December) ** Great Purification Festival ({{ill|lt=December 31st|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) ** New Year's Eve Festival ({{ill|lt=December 31st|Q2912|WD=Q2912}})

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amagoyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = Sangensha {{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}{{ill|lt=cypress bark roof|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} |Alternative name = Ikenomiya (Ikenomiya) |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=May 3rd|Q2547|WD=Q2547}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Otsuki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kotsuki Shrine Torii.JPG|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Otsuki Shrine'' (Otsuki Shrine {{Sfn|Otsuki Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}/Otsugi Jinja {{Sfn|Kotsuki Taisha/Kotsuki Shrine (Gods)|1986}}) is located in Aochi Town, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Kusatsu City|Q386677|WD=Q386677}}. In {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Divine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} is "Kakudari wisteria" [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=341 Otsuki Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Agency). === Enshrined God === The following two deities are enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Rakubetsuou|UNKNOWN|lt=Ochibetsu no Mikoto}} (Ochiwake no Mikoto) *: Also written as ``Rakubetsu no Mikoto'' or ``Sobetsu no Mikoto.'' He was the 11th {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}{{ill|lt=prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381}} and the ancestor of {{ill|lt=Kotsukiyama-kun|Q11462129|WD=Q11462129}} ({{ill|lt=Kotsuki clan|Q11462133|WD=Q11462133}}). Depending on the literature, the notation ``{{ill|lt=Issokubetsu no Mikoto|Q11492324|WD=Q11492324}}'' is also seen, but Issokubetsu no Mikoto is a half-brother of Ochibetsu no Mikoto. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amagoyane Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Amenokoyane Mikoto) *: {{ill|lt=Enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}} in {{ill|lt=Medieval|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}. He is the ancestral deity of the Fujiwara clan, and it has been suggested that there is a connection with the fact that the adjacent Kanakatsusho was the territory of {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559}} (Fujiwara clan's guardian deity) {{Sfn|Kotsuki Taisha/Kotsuki Shrine (Gods)|1986}}. === History === {{ill|lt=Ancient|Q41493|WD=Q41493}} in {{ill|lt=Kurita County|Q6446084|WD=Q6446084}} (current {{ill|lt=Kusatsu City|Q386677|WD=Q386677}}, {{jalink|Ritto City|Q It is said that the shrine was founded by {{ill|lt=Otsukiyama-kun|Q11462129|WD=Q11462129}} (Kozukiyama-kō), a powerful family from the 857310}} area, who enshrined {{ill|lt=Rakubetsu-ō|UNKNOWN|lt=Ochibetsu-no-Mikoto}} as the ancestral deity. Since Otsukiyama-kun presented {{ill|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)|Q30253177|WD=Q30253177|lt=Imperial Court}} with {{ill|lt=Ume|Q11644506|WD=Q11644506}}, it is assumed that he belonged to a family in the Kurita County {{ill|lt=Gunji|Q4152149|WD=Q4152149}} class. Otsukiyama-kun is {{ill|lt=Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan}} in {{ill|lt=Heiankyo|Q751907|WD=Q751907|lt=Kyoto}} in 15 years ({{ill|lt=873|Q30369|WD=Q30369}}) He later moved to the {{ill|lt=Kotsuki clan|Q11462133|WD=Q11462133}} ({{ill|lt=official|Q11452088|WD=Q11452088}} family) and became a member of the {{jalink|Imperial court}. (Japan)|Q30253177|Served the Imperial Court}}.{{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho}} is {{ill|lt=Omi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}{{jali nk|Kurita County|Q6446084}} is described as "Otsuki Shrine", and related shrines {{ill|lt=Kotsuki Taisha|Q11462126|WD=Q11462126}} ({{jalin {{Sfn|Kotsuki Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} along with Shimotoyama). The company name is pronounced ``Wotsukino''. In the shrine biography, it is said that in the 2nd year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714}}) he was given the proclamation of ``Shoichii Otsuki Daimyojin'', and also that {{ill|lt=Tentoku (Japan)|Q1327662|WD=Q1327662|lt=Tentoku}} In 3rd year ({{ill|lt=959|Q23750|WD=Q23750}}), it was relocated to the current location near Shizuike Pond, and it is said that it was called ``Ikenomiya'' (Ikenomiya), in contrast to ``Kozuenomiya'' of Kotsuki Taisha Shrine. The shrine buildings and sacred treasures were burnt down twice in {{ill|lt=Enkei (Japan)|Q1327068|WD=Q1327068|lt=Enkei}} and {{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397}}. {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}} to {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=During the Sengoku period}}, the shrine was worshiped by the {{ill|lt=Aochi clan|Q11661125|WD=Q11661125}}, and the shrine was donated and repaired, and it is said that a mikoshi was carried around the castle during the {{ill|lt=regular festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}}.Also, according to the shrine's legend, it has a close relationship with {{ill|lt=Koshioi Shrine|Q11462224|WD=Q11462224}} in Kusatsu City (Oji, Kusatsu City). Among them, {{ill|lt=Sadaharu|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153}}3 years ({{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shohei}} Since the construction of the mikoshi in {{ill|lt=1364|Q6185|WD=Q6185}} in 19, the mikoshi has traveled to Otsuki Taisha Shrine, Otsuki Taisha Shrine, and Koshioi Shrine during the annual festival, but when it was abolished, it is said that the sacred treasure of Koshioi Shrine, the bottle, was shared as proof of the relationship. [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=339 Koshioi Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Agency). The one at Otsuki Shrine was later destroyed in a fire, but it is said that it is still considered a sacred treasure at Koshioi Shrine. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}), {{ill|lt=Modern company ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}, and after extensive construction in Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}}), it was promoted to {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} in Meiji 16 ({{ill|lt=1883|Q7816|WD=Q7816}}). The current shrine building was constructed in 1896. === Precincts === The precincts adjoin the ruins of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aochi Castle|1=UNKNOWN}} from the Sengoku period. The main shrine is a three-room shrine {{ill|lt=nagaru-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}{{ill|lt=hinoki bark|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}}, with a frontage of three rooms and a depth of two rooms. It was constructed in 1896. The worship hall is {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}}, and has a frontage of three rooms and a depth of three rooms. It is also said that there were a group of ancient tombs within the precincts, and stone walls were built using the stones excavated from the tombs. In addition, the tree that is 18 meters tall and estimated to be over 100 years old is designated as a famous tree of Kusatsu City. Inside Tamagaki. * Kumano Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagison|1=UNKNOWN}}. Inside Tamagaki. * Hiyoshi Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Oyama Kuijin|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105}}. Inside Tamagaki. * Kifune Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Takamikami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takayokujin}}. {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} June 6th year ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707}}). Inside Tamagaki. * Ryuosha - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Watatsumi|Q1474187|WD=Q1474187|lt=Toyota Hikokami}}. It is said that in ancient times people would immerse the company's statue of the Dragon King in Shizu Pond to pray for rain. * Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}. {{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 5th year ({{ill|lt=863 years|Q25052|WD=Q25052}}) Kansho on August 15th. It is enshrined together with Nogami Shrine and is located near the torii gate. * Nogami Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Kayanohime|Q8191878|WD=Q8191878|lt=Kusanohime}}. It is enshrined together with Hachiman Shrine and is located near the torii gate. === Kyougaisha === * Hachimangu Shrine (Hachiman Shrine) ** Enshrinement location: Baba-cho, Kusatsu City ** Annual festival: {{ill|lt=January 10th|Q2243|WD=Q2243}} * Wakamiya Shrine ** Enshrinement location: Okamoto-cho, Kusatsu City ({{Coord|35|00|04.17|N|135|58|52.19|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Outside Shrine: Wakamiya Shrine}}) ** Enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Eight Wills and Life|1=UNKNOWN}} * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei Shrine) ** Enshrinement location: Aochi-cho, Kusatsu City (Shizuikewaki, {{Wiki coordinates|35|00|12.38|N|135|58|35.24|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|location|name=Shinmei Shrine}}) ** Enshrined deity: Owahime no Mikoto (same as {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}}) Wakamiya Shrine (Otsuki Shrine outside the precincts).JPG|Wakamiya Shrine (outside the precincts) Shinmei Shrine (Otsuki Shrine outside the precincts).JPG|Shinmei Shrine (outside the precincts of the shrine) === Festival ===
List of annual festivals
If there is no mention of large festival or middle festival, it is a small festival. * January ** Saitan Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1st|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) - Middle Festival. ** Dragon God Festival ({{ill|lt=January 15th|Q2260|WD=Q2260}}) *February ** Setsubun Festival ({{ill|lt=February 3rd|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}) - Middle Festival. ** Kigen Festival ({{ill|lt=February 11th|Q2335|WD=Q2335}}) ** Prayer Festival ({{ill|lt=February 23rd|Q2352|WD=Q2352}}) - Grand festival. *April ** Sakaki Festival ({{ill|lt=April 29th|Q2535|WD=Q2535}}) * May ** Yoimiya Festival ({{ill|lt=May 2nd|Q2545|WD=Q2545}}) ** Reitaisai ({{ill|lt=May 3rd|Q2547|WD=Q2547}}) - Taisai. ** Shinko Festival (May 3rd) **Saida Festival * June ** Summer Purification ({{ill|lt=June 30th|Q2657|WD=Q2657}}) * October ** Early Grain Festival ({{ill|lt=October 17th|Q2962|WD=Q2962}}) ** Kakeho Festival *November ** Shichigosan Festival ({{ill|lt=November 15th|Q2982|WD=Q2982}})** Niinamesai ({{ill|lt=November 23rd|Q3021|WD=Q3021}}) - Big festival. *December ** Cannabis card distribution ceremony (early December) ** Great Purification Festival ({{ill|lt=December 31st|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) ** New Year's Eve Festival ({{ill|lt=December 31st|Q2912|WD=Q2912}})

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amagoyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = Sangensha {{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}{{ill|lt=cypress bark roof|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} |Alternative name = Ikenomiya (Ikenomiya) |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=May 3rd|Q2547|WD=Q2547}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Otsuki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kotsuki Shrine Torii.JPG|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Otsuki Shrine'' (Otsuki Shrine {{Sfn|Otsuki Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}/Otsugi Jinja {{Sfn|Kotsuki Taisha/Kotsuki Shrine (Gods)|1986}}) is located in Aochi Town, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Kusatsu City|Q386677|WD=Q386677}}. In {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Divine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} is "Kakudari wisteria" [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=341 Otsuki Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Agency). === Enshrined God === The following two deities are enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Rakubetsuou|UNKNOWN|lt=Ochibetsu no Mikoto}} (Ochiwake no Mikoto) *: Also written as ``Rakubetsu no Mikoto'' or ``Sobetsu no Mikoto.'' He was the 11th {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}{{ill|lt=prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381}} and the ancestor of {{ill|lt=Kotsukiyama-kun|Q11462129|WD=Q11462129}} ({{ill|lt=Kotsuki clan|Q11462133|WD=Q11462133}}). Depending on the literature, the notation ``{{ill|lt=Issokubetsu no Mikoto|Q11492324|WD=Q11492324}}'' is also seen, but Issokubetsu no Mikoto is a half-brother of Ochibetsu no Mikoto. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amagoyane Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Amenokoyane Mikoto) *: {{ill|lt=Enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}} in {{ill|lt=Medieval|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}. He is the ancestral deity of the Fujiwara clan, and it has been suggested that there is a connection with the fact that the adjacent Kanakatsusho was the territory of {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559}} (Fujiwara clan's guardian deity) {{Sfn|Kotsuki Taisha/Kotsuki Shrine (Gods)|1986}}. === History === {{ill|lt=Ancient|Q41493|WD=Q41493}} in {{ill|lt=Kurita County|Q6446084|WD=Q6446084}} (current {{ill|lt=Kusatsu City|Q386677|WD=Q386677}}, {{jalink|Ritto City|Q It is said that the shrine was founded by {{ill|lt=Otsukiyama-kun|Q11462129|WD=Q11462129}} (Kozukiyama-kō), a powerful family from the 857310}} area, who enshrined {{ill|lt=Rakubetsu-ō|UNKNOWN|lt=Ochibetsu-no-Mikoto}} as the ancestral deity. Since Otsukiyama-kun presented {{ill|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)|Q30253177|WD=Q30253177|lt=Imperial Court}} with {{ill|lt=Ume|Q11644506|WD=Q11644506}}, it is assumed that he belonged to a family in the Kurita County {{ill|lt=Gunji|Q4152149|WD=Q4152149}} class. Otsukiyama-kun is {{ill|lt=Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan}} in {{ill|lt=Heiankyo|Q751907|WD=Q751907|lt=Kyoto}} in 15 years ({{ill|lt=873|Q30369|WD=Q30369}}) He later moved to the {{ill|lt=Kotsuki clan|Q11462133|WD=Q11462133}} ({{ill|lt=official|Q11452088|WD=Q11452088}} family) and became a member of the {{jalink|Imperial court}. (Japan)|Q30253177|Served the Imperial Court}}.{{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho}} is {{ill|lt=Omi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}{{jali nk|Kurita County|Q6446084}} is described as "Otsuki Shrine", and related shrines {{ill|lt=Kotsuki Taisha|Q11462126|WD=Q11462126}} ({{jalin {{Sfn|Kotsuki Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} along with Shimotoyama). The company name is pronounced ``Wotsukino''. In the shrine biography, it is said that in the 2nd year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714}}) he was given the proclamation of ``Shoichii Otsuki Daimyojin'', and also that {{ill|lt=Tentoku (Japan)|Q1327662|WD=Q1327662|lt=Tentoku}} In 3rd year ({{ill|lt=959|Q23750|WD=Q23750}}), it was relocated to the current location near Shizuike Pond, and it is said that it was called ``Ikenomiya'' (Ikenomiya), in contrast to ``Kozuenomiya'' of Kotsuki Taisha Shrine. The shrine buildings and sacred treasures were burnt down twice in {{ill|lt=Enkei (Japan)|Q1327068|WD=Q1327068|lt=Enkei}} and {{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397}}. {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}} to {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=During the Sengoku period}}, the shrine was worshiped by the {{ill|lt=Aochi clan|Q11661125|WD=Q11661125}}, and the shrine was donated and repaired, and it is said that a mikoshi was carried around the castle during the {{ill|lt=regular festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}}.Also, according to the shrine's legend, it has a close relationship with {{ill|lt=Koshioi Shrine|Q11462224|WD=Q11462224}} in Kusatsu City (Oji, Kusatsu City). Among them, {{ill|lt=Sadaharu|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153}}3 years ({{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shohei}} Since the construction of the mikoshi in {{ill|lt=1364|Q6185|WD=Q6185}} in 19, the mikoshi has traveled to Otsuki Taisha Shrine, Otsuki Taisha Shrine, and Koshioi Shrine during the annual festival, but when it was abolished, it is said that the sacred treasure of Koshioi Shrine, the bottle, was shared as proof of the relationship. [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=339 Koshioi Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Agency). The one at Otsuki Shrine was later destroyed in a fire, but it is said that it is still considered a sacred treasure at Koshioi Shrine. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}), {{ill|lt=Modern company ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}, and after extensive construction in Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}}), it was promoted to {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} in Meiji 16 ({{ill|lt=1883|Q7816|WD=Q7816}}). The current shrine building was constructed in 1896. === Precincts === The precincts adjoin the ruins of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aochi Castle|1=UNKNOWN}} from the Sengoku period. The main shrine is a three-room shrine {{ill|lt=nagaru-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}{{ill|lt=hinoki bark|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}}, with a frontage of three rooms and a depth of two rooms. It was constructed in 1896. The worship hall is {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}}, and has a frontage of three rooms and a depth of three rooms. It is also said that there were a group of ancient tombs within the precincts, and stone walls were built using the stones excavated from the tombs. In addition, the tree that is 18 meters tall and estimated to be over 100 years old is designated as a famous tree of Kusatsu City. Inside Tamagaki. * Kumano Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagison|1=UNKNOWN}}. Inside Tamagaki. * Hiyoshi Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Oyama Kuijin|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105}}. Inside Tamagaki. * Kifune Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Takamikami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takayokujin}}. {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} June 6th year ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707}}). Inside Tamagaki. * Ryuosha - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Watatsumi|Q1474187|WD=Q1474187|lt=Toyota Hikokami}}. It is said that in ancient times people would immerse the company's statue of the Dragon King in Shizu Pond to pray for rain. * Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}. {{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 5th year ({{ill|lt=863 years|Q25052|WD=Q25052}}) Kansho on August 15th. It is enshrined together with Nogami Shrine and is located near the torii gate. * Nogami Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Kayanohime|Q8191878|WD=Q8191878|lt=Kusanohime}}. It is enshrined together with Hachiman Shrine and is located near the torii gate. === Kyougaisha === * Hachimangu Shrine (Hachiman Shrine) ** Enshrinement location: Baba-cho, Kusatsu City ** Annual festival: {{ill|lt=January 10th|Q2243|WD=Q2243}} * Wakamiya Shrine ** Enshrinement location: Okamoto-cho, Kusatsu City ({{Coord|35|00|04.17|N|135|58|52.19|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Outside Shrine: Wakamiya Shrine}}) ** Enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Eight Wills and Life|1=UNKNOWN}} * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei Shrine) ** Enshrinement location: Aochi-cho, Kusatsu City (Shizuikewaki, {{Wiki coordinates|35|00|12.38|N|135|58|35.24|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|location|name=Shinmei Shrine}}) ** Enshrined deity: Owahime no Mikoto (same as {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}}) Wakamiya Shrine (Otsuki Shrine outside the precincts).JPG|Wakamiya Shrine (outside the precincts) Shinmei Shrine (Otsuki Shrine outside the precincts).JPG|Shinmei Shrine (outside the precincts of the shrine) === Festival ===
List of annual festivals
If there is no mention of large festival or middle festival, it is a small festival. * January ** Saitan Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1st|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) - Middle Festival. ** Dragon God Festival ({{ill|lt=January 15th|Q2260|WD=Q2260}}) *February ** Setsubun Festival ({{ill|lt=February 3rd|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}) - Middle Festival. ** Kigen Festival ({{ill|lt=February 11th|Q2335|WD=Q2335}}) ** Prayer Festival ({{ill|lt=February 23rd|Q2352|WD=Q2352}}) - Grand festival. *April ** Sakaki Festival ({{ill|lt=April 29th|Q2535|WD=Q2535}}) * May ** Yoimiya Festival ({{ill|lt=May 2nd|Q2545|WD=Q2545}}) ** Reitaisai ({{ill|lt=May 3rd|Q2547|WD=Q2547}}) - Taisai. ** Shinko Festival (May 3rd) **Saida Festival * June ** Summer Purification ({{ill|lt=June 30th|Q2657|WD=Q2657}}) * October ** Early Grain Festival ({{ill|lt=October 17th|Q2962|WD=Q2962}}) ** Kakeho Festival *November ** Shichigosan Festival ({{ill|lt=November 15th|Q2982|WD=Q2982}})** Niinamesai ({{ill|lt=November 23rd|Q3021|WD=Q3021}}) - Big festival. *December ** Cannabis card distribution ceremony (early December) ** Great Purification Festival ({{ill|lt=December 31st|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) ** New Year's Eve Festival ({{ill|lt=December 31st|Q2912|WD=Q2912}})

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amagoyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = Sangensha {{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}{{ill|lt=cypress bark roof|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}} |Alternative name = Ikenomiya (Ikenomiya) |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=May 3rd|Q2547|WD=Q2547}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Otsuki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kotsuki Shrine Torii.JPG|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Otsuki Shrine'' (Otsuki Shrine {{Sfn|Otsuki Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}/Otsugi Jinja {{Sfn|Kotsuki Taisha/Kotsuki Shrine (Gods)|1986}}) is located in Aochi Town, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Kusatsu City|Q386677|WD=Q386677}}. In {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Divine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} is "Kakudari wisteria" [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=341 Otsuki Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Agency). === Enshrined God === The following two deities are enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Rakubetsuou|UNKNOWN|lt=Ochibetsu no Mikoto}} (Ochiwake no Mikoto) *: Also written as ``Rakubetsu no Mikoto'' or ``Sobetsu no Mikoto.'' He was the 11th {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}}{{ill|lt=prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381}} and the ancestor of {{ill|lt=Kotsukiyama-kun|Q11462129|WD=Q11462129}} ({{ill|lt=Kotsuki clan|Q11462133|WD=Q11462133}}). Depending on the literature, the notation ``{{ill|lt=Issokubetsu no Mikoto|Q11492324|WD=Q11492324}}'' is also seen, but Issokubetsu no Mikoto is a half-brother of Ochibetsu no Mikoto. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amagoyane Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Amenokoyane Mikoto) *: {{ill|lt=Enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}} in {{ill|lt=Medieval|Q12554|WD=Q12554}}. He is the ancestral deity of the Fujiwara clan, and it has been suggested that there is a connection with the fact that the adjacent Kanakatsusho was the territory of {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559}} (Fujiwara clan's guardian deity) {{Sfn|Kotsuki Taisha/Kotsuki Shrine (Gods)|1986}}. === History === {{ill|lt=Ancient|Q41493|WD=Q41493}} in {{ill|lt=Kurita County|Q6446084|WD=Q6446084}} (current {{ill|lt=Kusatsu City|Q386677|WD=Q386677}}, {{jalink|Ritto City|Q It is said that the shrine was founded by {{ill|lt=Otsukiyama-kun|Q11462129|WD=Q11462129}} (Kozukiyama-kō), a powerful family from the 857310}} area, who enshrined {{ill|lt=Rakubetsu-ō|UNKNOWN|lt=Ochibetsu-no-Mikoto}} as the ancestral deity. Since Otsukiyama-kun presented {{ill|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)|Q30253177|WD=Q30253177|lt=Imperial Court}} with {{ill|lt=Ume|Q11644506|WD=Q11644506}}, it is assumed that he belonged to a family in the Kurita County {{ill|lt=Gunji|Q4152149|WD=Q4152149}} class. Otsukiyama-kun is {{ill|lt=Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan}} in {{ill|lt=Heiankyo|Q751907|WD=Q751907|lt=Kyoto}} in 15 years ({{ill|lt=873|Q30369|WD=Q30369}}) He later moved to the {{ill|lt=Kotsuki clan|Q11462133|WD=Q11462133}} ({{ill|lt=official|Q11452088|WD=Q11452088}} family) and became a member of the {{jalink|Imperial court}. (Japan)|Q30253177|Served the Imperial Court}}.{{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho}} is {{ill|lt=Omi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}{{jali nk|Kurita County|Q6446084}} is described as "Otsuki Shrine", and related shrines {{ill|lt=Kotsuki Taisha|Q11462126|WD=Q11462126}} ({{jalin {{Sfn|Kotsuki Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} along with Shimotoyama). The company name is pronounced ``Wotsukino''. In the shrine biography, it is said that in the 2nd year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714}}) he was given the proclamation of ``Shoichii Otsuki Daimyojin'', and also that {{ill|lt=Tentoku (Japan)|Q1327662|WD=Q1327662|lt=Tentoku}} In 3rd year ({{ill|lt=959|Q23750|WD=Q23750}}), it was relocated to the current location near Shizuike Pond, and it is said that it was called ``Ikenomiya'' (Ikenomiya), in contrast to ``Kozuenomiya'' of Kotsuki Taisha Shrine. The shrine buildings and sacred treasures were burnt down twice in {{ill|lt=Enkei (Japan)|Q1327068|WD=Q1327068|lt=Enkei}} and {{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397}}. {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}} to {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=During the Sengoku period}}, the shrine was worshiped by the {{ill|lt=Aochi clan|Q11661125|WD=Q11661125}}, and the shrine was donated and repaired, and it is said that a mikoshi was carried around the castle during the {{ill|lt=regular festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469}}.Also, according to the shrine's legend, it has a close relationship with {{ill|lt=Koshioi Shrine|Q11462224|WD=Q11462224}} in Kusatsu City (Oji, Kusatsu City). Among them, {{ill|lt=Sadaharu|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153}}3 years ({{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shohei}} Since the construction of the mikoshi in {{ill|lt=1364|Q6185|WD=Q6185}} in 19, the mikoshi has traveled to Otsuki Taisha Shrine, Otsuki Taisha Shrine, and Koshioi Shrine during the annual festival, but when it was abolished, it is said that the sacred treasure of Koshioi Shrine, the bottle, was shared as proof of the relationship. [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=339 Koshioi Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Agency). The one at Otsuki Shrine was later destroyed in a fire, but it is said that it is still considered a sacred treasure at Koshioi Shrine. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}}), {{ill|lt=Modern company ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}, and after extensive construction in Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}}), it was promoted to {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} in Meiji 16 ({{ill|lt=1883|Q7816|WD=Q7816}}). The current shrine building was constructed in 1896. === Precincts === The precincts adjoin the ruins of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aochi Castle|1=UNKNOWN}} from the Sengoku period. The main shrine is a three-room shrine {{ill|lt=nagaru-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}{{ill|lt=hinoki bark|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477}}, with a frontage of three rooms and a depth of two rooms. It was constructed in 1896. The worship hall is {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}}, and has a frontage of three rooms and a depth of three rooms. It is also said that there were a group of ancient tombs within the precincts, and stone walls were built using the stones excavated from the tombs. In addition, the tree that is 18 meters tall and estimated to be over 100 years old is designated as a famous tree of Kusatsu City. Inside Tamagaki. * Kumano Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagison|1=UNKNOWN}}. Inside Tamagaki. * Hiyoshi Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Oyama Kuijin|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105}}. Inside Tamagaki. * Kifune Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Takamikami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takayokujin}}. {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} June 6th year ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707}}). Inside Tamagaki. * Ryuosha - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Watatsumi|Q1474187|WD=Q1474187|lt=Toyota Hikokami}}. It is said that in ancient times people would immerse the company's statue of the Dragon King in Shizu Pond to pray for rain. * Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}. {{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 5th year ({{ill|lt=863 years|Q25052|WD=Q25052}}) Kansho on August 15th. It is enshrined together with Nogami Shrine and is located near the torii gate. * Nogami Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Kayanohime|Q8191878|WD=Q8191878|lt=Kusanohime}}. It is enshrined together with Hachiman Shrine and is located near the torii gate. === Kyougaisha === * Hachimangu Shrine (Hachiman Shrine) ** Enshrinement location: Baba-cho, Kusatsu City ** Annual festival: {{ill|lt=January 10th|Q2243|WD=Q2243}} * Wakamiya Shrine ** Enshrinement location: Okamoto-cho, Kusatsu City ({{Coord|35|00|04.17|N|135|58|52.19|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Outside Shrine: Wakamiya Shrine}}) ** Enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Eight Wills and Life|1=UNKNOWN}} * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei Shrine) ** Enshrinement location: Aochi-cho, Kusatsu City (Shizuikewaki, {{Wiki coordinates|35|00|12.38|N|135|58|35.24|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|location|name=Shinmei Shrine}}) ** Enshrined deity: Owahime no Mikoto (same as {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}}) Wakamiya Shrine (Otsuki Shrine outside the precincts).JPG|Wakamiya Shrine (outside the precincts) Shinmei Shrine (Otsuki Shrine outside the precincts).JPG|Shinmei Shrine (outside the precincts of the shrine) === Festival ===
List of annual festivals
If there is no mention of large festival or middle festival, it is a small festival. * January ** Saitan Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1st|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) - Middle Festival. ** Dragon God Festival ({{ill|lt=January 15th|Q2260|WD=Q2260}}) *February ** Setsubun Festival ({{ill|lt=February 3rd|Q2315|WD=Q2315}}) - Middle Festival. ** Kigen Festival ({{ill|lt=February 11th|Q2335|WD=Q2335}}) ** Prayer Festival ({{ill|lt=February 23rd|Q2352|WD=Q2352}}) - Grand festival. *April ** Sakaki Festival ({{ill|lt=April 29th|Q2535|WD=Q2535}}) * May ** Yoimiya Festival ({{ill|lt=May 2nd|Q2545|WD=Q2545}}) ** Reitaisai ({{ill|lt=May 3rd|Q2547|WD=Q2547}}) - Taisai. ** Shinko Festival (May 3rd) **Saida Festival * June ** Summer Purification ({{ill|lt=June 30th|Q2657|WD=Q2657}}) * October ** Early Grain Festival ({{ill|lt=October 17th|Q2962|WD=Q2962}}) ** Kakeho Festival *November ** Shichigosan Festival ({{ill|lt=November 15th|Q2982|WD=Q2982}})** Niinamesai ({{ill|lt=November 23rd|Q3021|WD=Q3021}}) - Big festival. *December ** Cannabis card distribution ceremony (early December) ** Great Purification Festival ({{ill|lt=December 31st|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) ** New Year's Eve Festival ({{ill|lt=December 31st|Q2912|WD=Q2912}})
=== Cultural assets === === Cultural property designated by Kusatsu City === *Tangible cultural property ** Wooden seated statue of the male god (sculpture) - Made in the late Heian period. {{ill|lt=Igoi Shrine|Q22117831|WD=Q22117831}}It has been pointed out that it resembles a statue of a male deity. Designated on July 27, 2017 (Heisei 29) {{PDFlink|[https://www.city.kusatsu.shiga.jp/shisei/shichoshitsu/kaikentaiwa/kishakaikei/h29/20170727.files/2-1.pdf Regarding the new designation as a tangible cultural property designated by Kusatsu City]}} (Kusatsu City homepage). [https://mainichi.jp/articles/20170829/ddl/k25/040/564000c "Three new municipal tangible cultural properties designated: late Heian period, wooden male god seated statues, Kusatsu Shiga"]{{broken link|date=July 2023}} (Mainichi Shimbun, August 29, 2017). . === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Kusatsu City|Q386677|WD=Q386677}}873 Aochi-cho ; access * '''Transportation''' ** {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}}{{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135}} ({{ill|lt=Tokaido Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152}})/JR Kusatsu Line{{jalink|Kusatsu Station (Shiga Prefecture)|Q775813|About 15 minutes by car or 50 minutes on foot from Kusatsu Station. ** From the same station, take the bus ({{ill|lt=Teisan Konan Kotsu|Q11481075|WD=Q11481075}}) and get off at the "Shizu Elementary School" stop, then walk for 5 minutes (*Shinmei Shrine is a 2-minute walk). * '''Automobile''' ** Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Shin-Meishin Expressway|Q7497118|WD=Q7497118}}{{ill|lt=Kusatsu-Takami Interchange|Q11618190|WD=Q11618190|lt=Kusatsu-Takami IC}}. ; around * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aochi Castle|1=UNKNOWN}} ruins * {{ill|lt=Kusatsu City Shizu Elementary School|Q88133486|WD=Q88133486}} === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book * Precinct information board * Dictionaries ** {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=Otsuki Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Japanese historical place name system|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 25 Place names in Shiga Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490255|ref={{Harvid|Otsuki Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}}} ** {{Cite Kobunkan|Q4346755|WD=Q4346755}}|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|Kotsuki clan (ancient clan)|2010}}}} * Other literature ** {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Kotsuki Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Meiji Shrine Materials|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Materials|1912}}}} *** [{{NDLDC|1088278/86}} "Meiji Shrine Journal Materials Fuken Gosha Middle"] (National Diet Library Digital Collection) See frames 86-87.** {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}edited|author=Nippo Uno|year=1986|chapter=Kotsuki Taisha/Kotsuki Shrine|title=Japanese Gods -Shrines and Sacred Sites- 5 book|author=Nobuyoshi Tanaka, Saburo Tamura, Yoshida Yanagiji |date=2002-04 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/2433/41936 |title=About math doctor Imao Otsuki (research on the history of mathematics) |CRID=1050282677273315968 |ref={{Harvid|About Math Doctor Imao Otsuki|2002}}}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Mr. Otsuki|Q11462133|WD=Q11462133}} * {{ill|lt=Kotsuki Taisha|Q11462126|WD=Q11462126}} {{wikidata link|Q11462132}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Otsuki Shrine |Image = [[File:Kotsuki Shrine Precincts.JPG|270px]]
Oratory (right)/Main shrine (back left) |Location = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kusatsu City|Q386677|WD=Q386677|1=Kusatsu, Shiga}}873 Aochicho |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|35|0|3.86|N|135|58|38.89|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kotsuki Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Rakubetsuou|UNKNOWN|lt=Ochibetsu no Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amagoyane no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = Sangensha {{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}{{ill|lt=cypress bark roof|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} |Alternative name = Ikenomiya (Ikenomiya) |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=May 3rd|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Otsuki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kotsuki Shrine Torii.JPG|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Otsuki Shrine'' (Otsuki Shrine {{Sfn|Otsuki Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}/Otsugi Jinja {{Sfn|Kotsuki Taisha/Kotsuki Shrine (Gods)|1986}}) is located in Aochi Town, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kusatsu City|Q386677|WD=Q386677|1=Kusatsu, Shiga}}. In {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Divine crest|1=UNKNOWN}} is "Kakudari wisteria" [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=341 Otsuki Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Agency). === Enshrined God === The following two deities are enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Rakubetsuou|UNKNOWN|lt=Ochibetsu no Mikoto}} (Ochiwake no Mikoto) *: Also written as ``Rakubetsu no Mikoto'' or ``Sobetsu no Mikoto.'' He was the 11th {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}{{ill|lt=prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381|1=Imperial Prince}} and the ancestor of {{ill|lt=Kotsukiyama-kun|Q11462129|WD=Q11462129|1=Kotsukiyama-kun}} ({{ill|lt=Kotsuki clan|Q11462133|WD=Q11462133|1=Ozuki clan}}). Depending on the literature, the notation ``{{ill|lt=Issokubetsu no Mikoto|Q11492324|WD=Q11492324|1=Prince Ikobayawake no Mikoto}}'' is also seen, but Issokubetsu no Mikoto is a half-brother of Ochibetsu no Mikoto. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amagoyane Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Amenokoyane Mikoto) *: {{ill|lt=Enshrined|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}} in {{ill|lt=Medieval|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}. He is the ancestral deity of the Fujiwara clan, and it has been suggested that there is a connection with the fact that the adjacent Kanakatsusho was the territory of {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|1=Kasuga-taisha}} (Fujiwara clan's guardian deity) {{Sfn|Kotsuki Taisha/Kotsuki Shrine (Gods)|1986}}. === History === {{ill|lt=Ancient|Q41493|WD=Q41493|1=Ancient history}} in {{ill|lt=Kurita County|Q6446084|WD=Q6446084|1=Kurita District, Shiga}} (current {{ill|lt=Kusatsu City|Q386677|WD=Q386677|1=Kusatsu, Shiga}}, {{jalink|Ritto City|Q It is said that the shrine was founded by {{ill|lt=Otsukiyama-kun|Q11462129|WD=Q11462129|1=Otsukiyama-kun}} (Kozukiyama-kō), a powerful family from the 857310}} area, who enshrined {{ill|lt=Rakubetsu-ō|UNKNOWN|lt=Ochibetsu-no-Mikoto}} as the ancestral deity. Since Otsukiyama-kun presented {{ill|lt=Imperial Court (Japan)|Q30253177|WD=Q30253177|lt=Imperial Court|1=Imperial Court of Japan}} with {{ill|lt=Ume|Q11644506|WD=Q11644506|1=Uneme}}, it is assumed that he belonged to a family in the Kurita County {{ill|lt=Gunji|Q4152149|WD=Q4152149|1=Gunji}} class. Otsukiyama-kun is {{ill|lt=Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan|1=Jōgan}} in {{ill|lt=Heiankyo|Q751907|WD=Q751907|lt=Kyoto|1=Heian-kyō}} in 15 years ({{ill|lt=873|Q30369|WD=Q30369|1=873}}) He later moved to the {{ill|lt=Kotsuki clan|Q11462133|WD=Q11462133|1=Ozuki clan}} ({{ill|lt=official|Q11452088|WD=Q11452088|1=official}} family) and became a member of the {{jalink|Imperial court}. (Japan)|Q30253177|Served the Imperial Court}}.{{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended|1=Enchō}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} is {{ill|lt=Omi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}{{jali nk|Kurita County|Q6446084}} is described as "Otsuki Shrine", and related shrines {{ill|lt=Kotsuki Taisha|Q11462126|WD=Q11462126|1=Otsuki-taisha}} ({{jalin {{Sfn|Kotsuki Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} along with Shimotoyama). The company name is pronounced ``Wotsukino''. In the shrine biography, it is said that in the 2nd year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|WD=Q24714|1=860}}) he was given the proclamation of ``Shoichii Otsuki Daimyojin'', and also that {{ill|lt=Tentoku (Japan)|Q1327662|WD=Q1327662|lt=Tentoku|1=Tentoku}} In 3rd year ({{ill|lt=959|Q23750|WD=Q23750|1=959}}), it was relocated to the current location near Shizuike Pond, and it is said that it was called ``Ikenomiya'' (Ikenomiya), in contrast to ``Kozuenomiya'' of Kotsuki Taisha Shrine. The shrine buildings and sacred treasures were burnt down twice in {{ill|lt=Enkei (Japan)|Q1327068|WD=Q1327068|lt=Enkei|1=Enkyō (Kamakura period)}} and {{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}}. {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} to {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=During the Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the shrine was worshiped by the {{ill|lt=Aochi clan|Q11661125|WD=Q11661125|1=Aochi clan}}, and the shrine was donated and repaired, and it is said that a mikoshi was carried around the castle during the {{ill|lt=regular festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}}.Also, according to the shrine's legend, it has a close relationship with {{ill|lt=Koshioi Shrine|Q11462224|WD=Q11462224|1=Oshioi Shrine}} in Kusatsu City (Oji, Kusatsu City). Among them, {{ill|lt=Sadaharu|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153|1=Jōji}}3 years ({{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1143587|WD=Q1143587|lt=Shohei|1=Shōhei}} Since the construction of the mikoshi in {{ill|lt=1364|Q6185|WD=Q6185|1=1364}} in 19, the mikoshi has traveled to Otsuki Taisha Shrine, Otsuki Taisha Shrine, and Koshioi Shrine during the annual festival, but when it was abolished, it is said that the sacred treasure of Koshioi Shrine, the bottle, was shared as proof of the relationship. [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=339 Koshioi Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Agency). The one at Otsuki Shrine was later destroyed in a fire, but it is said that it is still considered a sacred treasure at Koshioi Shrine. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}), {{ill|lt=Modern company ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, and after extensive construction in Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}}), it was promoted to {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in Meiji 16 ({{ill|lt=1883|Q7816|WD=Q7816|1=1883}}). The current shrine building was constructed in 1896. === Precincts === The precincts adjoin the ruins of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aochi Castle|1=UNKNOWN}} from the Sengoku period. The main shrine is a three-room shrine {{ill|lt=nagaru-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}{{ill|lt=hinoki bark|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}}, with a frontage of three rooms and a depth of two rooms. It was constructed in 1896. The worship hall is {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}}, and has a frontage of three rooms and a depth of three rooms. It is also said that there were a group of ancient tombs within the precincts, and stone walls were built using the stones excavated from the tombs. In addition, the tree that is 18 meters tall and estimated to be over 100 years old is designated as a famous tree of Kusatsu City. Otsuki Shrine Main Hall.JPG|Main Hall Otsuki Shrine Hall of Worship.JPG | Hall of Worship Otsuki Shrine Stone wall of the ancient tomb.JPG|Stone wall of the ancient tomb Shizu Pond.JPG | Shizu Pond === Setsusha === === Precinct Company === * Ebisu Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshiro Nushikami|1=UNKNOWN}}. Inside Tamagaki. * Kumano Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagison|1=UNKNOWN}}. Inside Tamagaki. * Hiyoshi Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Oyama Kuijin|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}. Inside Tamagaki. * Kifune Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Takamikami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Takayokujin|1=Kuraokami}}. {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} June 6th year ({{ill|lt=1578|Q6707|WD=Q6707|1=1578}}). Inside Tamagaki. * Ryuosha - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Watatsumi|Q1474187|WD=Q1474187|lt=Toyota Hikokami|1=Watatsumi}}. It is said that in ancient times people would immerse the company's statue of the Dragon King in Shizu Pond to pray for rain. * Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. {{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 5th year ({{ill|lt=863 years|Q25052|WD=Q25052|1=863}}) Kansho on August 15th. It is enshrined together with Nogami Shrine and is located near the torii gate. * Nogami Shrine - Deity enshrined: {{ill|lt=Kayanohime|Q8191878|WD=Q8191878|lt=Kusanohime|1=Kaya-no-hime}}. It is enshrined together with Hachiman Shrine and is located near the torii gate. === Kyougaisha === * Hachimangu Shrine (Hachiman Shrine) ** Enshrinement location: Baba-cho, Kusatsu City ** Annual festival: {{ill|lt=January 10th|Q2243|WD=Q2243|1=January 10}} * Wakamiya Shrine ** Enshrinement location: Okamoto-cho, Kusatsu City ({{Coord|35|00|04.17|N|135|58|52.19|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Outside Shrine: Wakamiya Shrine}}) ** Enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Eight Wills and Life|1=UNKNOWN}} * Shinmei Shrine (Shinmei Shrine) ** Enshrinement location: Aochi-cho, Kusatsu City (Shizuikewaki, {{Wiki coordinates|35|00|12.38|N|135|58|35.24|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline|location|name=Shinmei Shrine}}) ** Enshrined deity: Owahime no Mikoto (same as {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=Amaterasu}}) Wakamiya Shrine (Otsuki Shrine outside the precincts).JPG|Wakamiya Shrine (outside the precincts) Shinmei Shrine (Otsuki Shrine outside the precincts).JPG|Shinmei Shrine (outside the precincts of the shrine) === Festival ===
List of annual festivals
If there is no mention of large festival or middle festival, it is a small festival. * January ** Saitan Festival ({{ill|lt=January 1st|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}}) - Middle Festival. ** Dragon God Festival ({{ill|lt=January 15th|Q2260|WD=Q2260|1=January 15}}) *February ** Setsubun Festival ({{ill|lt=February 3rd|Q2315|WD=Q2315|1=February 3}}) - Middle Festival. ** Kigen Festival ({{ill|lt=February 11th|Q2335|WD=Q2335|1=February 11}}) ** Prayer Festival ({{ill|lt=February 23rd|Q2352|WD=Q2352|1=February 23}}) - Grand festival. *April ** Sakaki Festival ({{ill|lt=April 29th|Q2535|WD=Q2535|1=April 29}}) * May ** Yoimiya Festival ({{ill|lt=May 2nd|Q2545|WD=Q2545|1=May 2}}) ** Reitaisai ({{ill|lt=May 3rd|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}}) - Taisai. ** Shinko Festival (May 3rd) **Saida Festival * June ** Summer Purification ({{ill|lt=June 30th|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}}) * October ** Early Grain Festival ({{ill|lt=October 17th|Q2962|WD=Q2962|1=October 17}}) ** Kakeho Festival *November ** Shichigosan Festival ({{ill|lt=November 15th|Q2982|WD=Q2982|1=November 15}})** Niinamesai ({{ill|lt=November 23rd|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) - Big festival. *December ** Cannabis card distribution ceremony (early December) ** Great Purification Festival ({{ill|lt=December 31st|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}}) ** New Year's Eve Festival ({{ill|lt=December 31st|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}})
